script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'forensic3-race-runs',
  description: 'Sol + Fable + K3 independently forensically audit 4 task-72 reports (A gate/faith-off 0.399, B gate/faith-on 0.361, champ_ourcorpus 0.367, step3 best-champion 0.429) LINE BY LINE to explain the RACE gap; Opus consolidates.',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Audit', detail: 'Fable + Codex Sol + Kimi K3 independently audit the 4 scored texts line-by-line' },
    { title: 'Consolidate', detail: 'Opus merges into one forensic answer, line by line' },
  ],
}
const REV = "/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/forensic3"
const PACK = `${REV}/pack.md`
const BRIEF = `You are forensically auditing FOUR task-72 deep-research literature-review reports to explain their RACE scores. The prompt: "a literature review on the restructuring impact of AI on the labor market." RACE dimension WEIGHTS: Insight 0.32, Comprehensiveness 0.29, Instruction-Following 0.25, Readability 0.14. The four SCORED texts (verbatim, as the GPT judge saw them) + the score table + verified confounds are ALL in the CONTEXT PACK. The judge's own reasoning was NOT saved — you must audit the TEXTS themselves.

Scores: A (our gate, faithfulness OFF) 0.3992 | B (our gate, faithfulness ON) 0.3610 | champ_ourcorpus (champion pipeline on OUR corpus) 0.3671 | step3_rescore_r2 (BEST available champion) 0.4291.

Do a LINE-BY-LINE forensic audit — QUOTE specific passages/sentences from the pack for every claim. Answer:

1. WHY does STEP3 (best champion, 0.429, 3372 words) beat A (our best, 0.399, 5201 words), dimension by dimension — especially Insight (0.414 vs 0.384) and Readability (0.426 vs 0.348)? Quote concrete differences: the TITLE, the OPENING paragraph, section structure, paragraph shape, how each SYNTHESIZES across sources vs lists facts, citation style, and CONCISENESS (step3 is 1830 words SHORTER yet scores higher). What specifically does step3 DO that A does not?

2. Is A>B (0.399 vs 0.361) actually the FAITHFULNESS GHOST, or the confounds (A and B had different corpora/contracts/length + both carry the D8 banner)? Read A and B line-by-line: what did B's faithfulness-ON verification visibly REMOVE, hedge, or gap-state that A kept? Is B thinner in insight/more hedged, or just a different draft? Is the 0.038 delta credibly the ghost, or noise? Be honest about attribution.

3. Our gate (A) BEATS champ_ourcorpus (0.399 vs 0.367) on the SAME corpus — what does our gate composition do RIGHT? And champ_ourcorpus (champion, 2563 words) scores far below step3 (champion, 3372 words) — is that a CORPUS-quality gap or a COMPOSITION gap?

4. THE CHROME DRAGS — quantify the plausible RACE cost of: (a) our title being the RAW PROMPT ("# Research report: Please write a literature review on...") vs champion's clean "# A literature review on..."; (b) the D8 "UNVERIFIED-by-D8" BANNER present in our scored text; (c) length bloat (5201/4549 vs 3372/2563). Would fixing just these plausibly close the gap to step3?

5. RANK the concrete fixes by RACE leverage (expected points per unit effort). Name the SINGLE highest-leverage change.

Be brutally specific; quote the text. This is forensics, not a summary. Find what a superficial reading would MISS.`

phase('Audit')
const audits = await parallel([
  () => agent(`You are FABLE 5, forensic auditor at MAX depth. Read the pack at ${PACK} IN FULL (all four scored texts), then do the line-by-line audit below. Quote specific passages. Write your full audit to ${REV}/fable.md and return a tight summary (your single highest-leverage finding + the honest verdict on whether A>B is the ghost).\n\n===== BRIEF =====\n${BRIEF}`,
    { label: 'fable', phase: 'Audit', model: 'fable', effort: 'high' }),
  () => agent(`You are the CODEX-SOL RUNNER. Get Codex (GPT-5.6) at reasoning-effort HIGH to produce the forensic audit, captured to a file.
1. Build ${REV}/combined_prompt.txt = "You are a forensic auditor at maximum depth. ALL four scored texts + scores + confounds are embedded below; quote them by passage. Do NOT read files.\\n\\n===== CONTEXT PACK =====\\n" + contents of ${PACK} + "\\n\\n===== BRIEF =====\\n" + the brief below.
2. Run: cd /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent && timeout 1500 codex exec --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -c model_reasoning_effort="high" - < ${REV}/combined_prompt.txt > ${REV}/codex.md 2> ${REV}/codex.err ; echo "EXIT $?"
3. If codex.md is empty/errored/timed out, report honestly with whatever landed; do NOT fabricate. Return a tight summary of what Codex actually said (or that it failed) + the path.\n\n===== BRIEF (put in combined_prompt.txt) =====\n${BRIEF}`,
    { label: 'codex-sol', phase: 'Audit', effort: 'high' }),
  () => agent(`You are the KIMI-K3 RUNNER. Get Kimi K3 (via OpenRouter) to produce the forensic audit, captured to a file.
1. Write ${REV}/run_kimi.py: read OPENROUTER_API_KEY from /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.env (regex ^OPENROUTER_API_KEY=(.+)$, strip quotes); read ${PACK}; POST to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions model "moonshotai/kimi-k3", max_tokens 60000, temperature 0.3, 1500s timeout, retry x5 backoff; system="You are Kimi K3, a forensic auditor at maximum depth; quote passages; find what a superficial reading misses"; user = pack text + the brief below; capture msg.get("content") OR msg.get("reasoning"); write to ${REV}/kimi.md.
2. Run python3 ${REV}/run_kimi.py . If empty/failed, report honestly; do NOT fabricate. Return a tight summary + path.\n\n===== BRIEF =====\n${BRIEF}`,
    { label: 'kimi-k3', phase: 'Audit', effort: 'high' }),
])

phase('Consolidate')
const verdict = await agent(`You are OPUS consolidating a 3-model forensic audit of 4 task-72 reports into ONE line-by-line answer for the operator. Read: the pack ${PACK}; ${REV}/fable.md; ${REV}/codex.md (may be partial/failed — say so honestly, attribute nothing false); ${REV}/kimi.md (same). Note which models actually returned.
Produce the operator's forensic answer:
1. THE VERDICT on A>B: is it the faithfulness ghost, or confounds? Give the honest, evidence-quoted answer (converge/diverge across models).
2. WHY step3 (0.429) beats our best A (0.399): the concrete, quoted differences (title, opening, structure, synthesis vs listing, conciseness, no-banner). Dimension by dimension.
3. WHY our gate A BEATS champ_ourcorpus (0.367) on the same corpus — what the gate does right.
4. THE CHROME DRAGS quantified: raw-prompt title, D8 banner, length bloat — and whether fixing them closes the gap.
5. RANKED fix list by RACE leverage + the SINGLE highest-leverage change.
6. Where the 3 models DISAGREED and your adjudication.
Quote specific passages throughout. Write the full consolidated audit to /home/polaris/polaris_project/FORENSIC3_AUDIT.md and return a structured summary.`,
  { label: 'consolidate', phase: 'Consolidate', effort: 'high', schema: {
    type:'object', additionalProperties:false,
    required:['models_returned','agtb_verdict','why_step3_beats_A','why_A_beats_champourcorpus','chrome_drags','ranked_fixes','single_highest_leverage','disagreements','plan_path','headline'],
    properties:{
      models_returned:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}},
      agtb_verdict:{type:'string'}, why_step3_beats_A:{type:'string'}, why_A_beats_champourcorpus:{type:'string'},
      chrome_drags:{type:'string'}, ranked_fixes:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, single_highest_leverage:{type:'string'},
      disagreements:{type:'array',items:{type:'string'}}, plan_path:{type:'string'}, headline:{type:'string'},
    },
  } })

return verdict

models_returned: Kimi / kimi-k2.6 (DID NOT RETURN — kimi.md absent on disk; run_kimi.py present but produced no output; nothing attributed to it)
result.agtb_verdict: CONFOUNDED, not a clean faithfulness measurement. Both models that returned (Fable, Codex) agree: the faithfulness-cost MECHANISM is confirmed in B's text (147 removals incl. 66 entailment_failed vs A's 56/zero; stub 2-3 sentence sections; orphaned anaphora 'The BLS corroborates this productivity slowdown'/'However, this process'/'The consequence has been' as cut-marks; the BLS 0.6pp stat quadruplicated because strict_verify vetoed all 44 dedup rewrites) but the 0.038 MAGNITUDE is NOT attributable to it. Codex: 'It could contain a faithfulness penalty; it cannot quantify one.' A and B differ on every axis but title/banner: corpora 234 vs 302, contracts 12 vs 8-9 hard terms, outlines 6 vs 9 sections, length 5201 vs 4549w, tier 60% vs 74% T4, n=1 each. Honest ghost share: ~0.01-0.03 of 0.038, rest is corpus/contract/length noise. Do NOT book 0.038 as the price of faithfulness.
result.why_step3_beats_A: Gap 0.0299. Readability owns 36% (+0.0778 raw, lowest-weighted dim), Insight 32%, Comp 27%, IF only 6% (essentially TIED 0.4288 vs 0.4361 — it is NOT an instruction-following loss). TITLE: A ships the raw prompt '# Research report: Please write a literature review... Ensure the review only cites...' vs step3's '# A literature review on...'. OPENING: A's first sentence is grammatically broken (three interrogatives mashed with terminal '?.') after the self-indicting D8 banner; step3 opens with Klaus-Schwab framing + a real review roadmap. THE DEFERRAL-POINTER DISEASE (decisive, missed on a shallow read): ~41 pointer sentences (~800w, ~15% of body) say 'X is covered under Y'; the Synthesis section is ~13/16 pointers and synthesizes nothing; 3 of 6 Key Findings are cross-references. SYNTHESIS vs LISTING: step3 names+stitches literature (Autor 1940-2018 'reinforcing the view', Noy/Eloundou/Tomlinson/Cazzaniga), adjudicates sources, interprets numbers ('workers save time but do not on average produce more'); A machine-guns anonymized stats ('A study of 671 occupations','One study') and its Tension callouts copy body sentences. CONCISENESS: A is 5201w vs 3372w (54% longer) yet loses Comp 0.414<0.442 because the extra words are anti-content. BROKEN ARTIFACTS in A's scored text: truncated Tension '...using U.S.', shard findings ('28015, Spain.','Private domestic final purchases have been solid.'), astro-ph/gr-qc contradiction subjects + SHA-256 in the appendix.
result.why_A_beats_champourcorpus: Same corpus, so the +0.032 is pure composition. A won IF +0.057, Insight +0.043, Comp +0.021; lost only Readability -0.016. Our gate's eligibility firewall (29 sources masked) + 12-hard-term contract keep the composition relentlessly on-prompt (task mechanisms, robot-displacement vs firm +6% growth, Korea 2018-2023, WEF, UK/Amazon/Singapore/Germany policy). The champion pipeline has NO firewall, so on our junk corpus it composed the junk: verbatim back-to-back sentence repetition ('By 2026, jobs that require social and cognitive skills...' twice), a phantom 'Table 2 in three main ways' that never materializes, unexplained 'R2 of 0.9325 XGBoost', and off-topic bleed (COVID worker-anxiety p<0.001, $274B healthcare waste, construction cost ratios 1.03-1.54). On equal corpus our gate BEATS the champion on all three packaging-independent dimensions and loses only Readability (champ has the clean title + no telemetry appendix). Real composition advantage, not just a corpus artifact.
result.chrome_drags: Forensic ranges, not causal estimates (no randomized renderer test). (a) RAW-PROMPT TITLE: ~0.001-0.010, mostly IF/Read, ceiling small since A's IF is only -0.007 vs step3. (b) D8 BANNER ('findings are UNVERIFIED-by-D8... Treat them as UNVERIFIED... pending a re-judge'): ~0.001-0.015, Codex allows near-zero or even a transparency reward; CANCELS in A-B since both carry it and A still hit IF 0.4288 with it. (c) LENGTH BLOAT (the big one, mislabeled — it is ANTI-content: ~41 deferral pointers ~800w + junk shards + ~1000w telemetry appendix with astro-ph/SHA-256/evaluator-override): ~0.006-0.020, owns most of the -0.078 Read gap + a share of Insight. DOES FIXING CLOSE THE GAP? Only partially if cosmetic: Read+IF advantages = ~0.0127 but Insight+Comp = ~0.0177 remain even with title/banner/format perfected. Plausible landing ~0.408-0.425 (within noise of the champion) but NOT reliably past 0.429 without actual synthesis reconstruction. Codex: 'the winning action is not make it shorter; it is replace non-analytical prose with analytical prose.'
ranked_fixes: 1. Kill the deferral-pointer mechanism (delete relocated facts, never emit 'X is covered under Y'; rebuild Key Findings + let Synthesis synthesize): +0.010-0.018, one composer/dedup rule — highest TOTAL leverage

2. Strip non-report chrome from the SCORED text (D8 banner, telemetry appendix, single-source shard block -> sidecar; keep only short Limitations): +0.008-0.018, near-zero effort — best points-per-effort / highest-ROI quick win
3. Regenerate after verification instead of accepting deletion stubs (fixes B's 2-sentence sections + orphaned anaphora): +0.006-0.018
4. Clean title + fix the broken framing/'?.' sentence + truncated Tension fragment: +0.002-0.008, trivial
5. Named-author citation + require >=1 cross-source adjudication sentence per section: +0.004-0.010 (Insight)
6. Enforce journal-only source instruction before composition (drop WEF/McKinsey/'Speech by Governor Barr'): +0.006-0.015 (IF) — Codex ranks this separately, Fable folds it into corpus
7. Corpus canon retrieval (JEP/NBER/ILO/OECD): largest absolute lever (~0.05 handicap) but most effort
result.single_highest_leverage: Codex's synthesis-first paragraph compositor — replace the 'section = list of verified claims + cross-references' machine with paragraphs structured as (1) analytical proposition, (2) evidence from >=2 studies, (3) agreement/disagreement, (4) WHY results differ, (5) implication. Expected +0.015-0.030. It STRICTLY CONTAINS Fable's cheaper 'delete the deferral pointers' fix (a synthesis-first paragraph has no place to emit a pointer) and is the ONLY change with a path ABOVE 0.429 rather than merely up to it, because it closes the residual ~0.0177 Insight+Comp deficit that packaging alone cannot touch. Sequencing: do Fable's delete-pointers + strip-chrome in week 1 (near-free path to ~0.42, within champion noise, de-risks the gap), then ship the synthesis-first compositor to actually clear step3.
disagreements: Ghost magnitude in A-B: Fable bounds it (~0.01-0.03); Codex refuses to bound it (n=1, >=6 confounds). Adjudication: Codex more rigorous; Fable's range is a reasonable upper-bounded prior not an identification claim — report as 'ghost real, <=~0.03, unidentified' (both compatible).

Chrome-cost ranges: Fable higher (banner ~0.005-0.015), Codex lower (~0.001-0.005, possibly near-zero). Adjudication: Codex's floor better-reasoned (banner cancels in A-B, A scored IF 0.4288 with it); report the union ~0.001-0.015 and flag it.

Highest-leverage framing: Fable 'delete pointers' (narrow/cheap/->~0.42) vs Codex 'synthesis-first compositor' (broad/->potentially >0.429). Adjudication: same lever two altitudes, sequence them — not a real conflict.

Journal-only-source fix: Codex ranks it as its own IF lever (+0.006-0.015, cites A's 60% T4 + 'Speech by Governor Barr'); Fable folds source-quality into the corpus lever. Adjudication: Codex right to surface it, but note neither report actually complied (step3 also leans on WEF/PwC/IBM — it just LOOKS compliant via author-year); kept as ranked fix #6.
Kimi is an ABSENCE not a disagreement — the anticipated 3-way adjudication is a 2-way one; nothing attributed to Kimi.
result.headline: 2 of 3 models returned (Fable + Codex; Kimi no-showed) and they independently converge: A>B is CONFOUNDED (ghost mechanism confirmed in B's 147-removal scars, but 0.038 magnitude unidentified — n=1, corpora/contracts/tiers all differ, honest ghost share ~0.01-0.03). step3 beats A mainly on Readability (36% of a 0.030 gap) driven by A's deferral-pointer disease (~41 pointer sentences, a Synthesis that is 13/16 pointers, half its Key Findings are cross-references) + telemetry appendix + broken shards; IF is essentially tied. Our gate BEATS champ_ourcorpus on the same corpus (+0.032, winning Comp/Insight/IF via the eligibility firewall) and loses only Readability — proving the champion's edge is corpus + packaging, not composition. Chrome fixes plausibly reach ~0.42 (within champion noise) but the residual ~0.0177 Insight+Comp needs the single highest-leverage change: a synthesis-first compositor, NOT turning faithfulness off.

summary: Sol + Fable + K3 independently forensically audit 4 task-72 reports (A gate/faith-off 0.399, B gate/faith-on 0.361, champ_ourcorpus 0.367, step3 best-champion 0.429) LINE BY LINE to explain the RACE gap; Opus consolidates.
